app.title: Chinese Bridge Culture
app.subtitle: A millennium of engineering · A digital spatio-temporal archive

poem.originalLanguageNote: The following lines are quoted in classical Chinese (original text).

analysis.title: 🔗 Correlation analysis
analysis.routeCanalDesc: Fully connected in 1293 (Yuan dynasty), stretching 1,794 km from Beijing to Hangzhou — the longest artificial waterway in the world
analysis.routeSilkDesc: A major east-west trade route formed after Zhang Qian's missions, connecting Chang'an to Central Asia via the Hexi Corridor
analysis.routeTeaNDesc: Northern tea-horse route from Chengdu through Ya'an and Luding Bridge to Lhasa
analysis.routeTeaSDesc: Southern tea-horse route from Kunming through Dali and Lijiang to Lhasa
analysis.riverYangtzeDesc: Over 6,300 km long, China's longest river, flowing through 11 provinces from Qinghai to Shanghai
analysis.riverHuangheDesc: 5,464 km long, cradle of Chinese civilization, flowing through 9 provinces from Qinghai to Shandong
analysis.waterwayLingquDesc: Built in 214 BC under Qin Shi Huang, connecting the Xiang and Li rivers — one of the world's oldest canals
analysis.waterwayHangouDesc: Dug by King Fuchai of Wu in 486 BC, linking the Yangtze and Huai rivers — the earliest section of the Grand Canal
analysis.waterwayTongjiDesc: Built in 605 AD by Emperor Yang of Sui, running from Luoyang through Kaifeng to the Huai River — the core of the Sui-Tang Grand Canal
analysis.waterwayYongjiDesc: Built in 608 AD, running north from Luoyang to Zhuojun (modern Beijing) — a supply route for the Goguryeo campaigns
analysis.waterwayJiangnanDesc: Completed in 610 AD, from Zhenjiang through Suzhou to Hangzhou — the lifeline of the prosperous Jiangnan region
analysis.waterwayHuitongDesc: Built in 1289 under the Yuan dynasty, cutting through Shandong to straighten the canal route bypassing Luoyang
analysis.waterwayBeiyunDesc: Opened in 1293, running from Tianjin to Dadu (Beijing) — the northernmost section of the Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal
analysis.waterwayZhongyunDesc: Built in the Yuan dynasty, connecting Huai'an to Jining — a direct north-south replacement for the Sui-Tang Tongji Canal
analysis.waterwayZhedongDesc: Originating around 490 BC, from Hangzhou through Shaoxing to Ningbo — connecting the Qiantang and Yong rivers
analysis.riverZhujiangDesc: 2,320 km long, the largest river in southern China, flowing through Yunnan, Guizhou, Guangxi and Guangdong into the South China Sea
analysis.riverHuaiheDesc: 1,000 km long, the geographic boundary between north and south China, flowing through Henan, Anhui and Jiangsu
analysis.riverHaiheDesc: The largest river system in North China, converging five major tributaries at Tianjin before reaching the Bohai Sea
analysis.riverSonghuaDesc: 1,927 km long, the largest tributary of the Amur River, flowing through Jilin and Heilongjiang provinces
analysis.riverLiaoheDesc: 1,345 km long, the main river of southern Manchuria, flowing through Inner Mongolia, Jilin and Liaoning
analysis.riverHanjiangDesc: 1,577 km long, the longest tributary of the Yangtze, flowing through Shaanxi and Hubei provinces
analysis.riverGanjiangDesc: 766 km long, the largest river in Jiangxi province, flowing north into Poyang Lake
analysis.riverXiangjiangDesc: 856 km long, the largest river in Hunan province, flowing north into Dongting Lake
analysis.riverMinjiangDesc: 577 km long, the largest river in Fujian province, flowing through Nanping to Fuzhou and into the sea
analysis.riverQiantangDesc: 688 km long, the largest river in Zhejiang, famous worldwide for its spectacular tidal bore
analysis.riverWeiheDesc: 818 km long, the largest tributary of the Yellow River, the mother river of the Guanzhong Plain in Shaanxi
analysis.riverLuoheDesc: 447 km long, an important southern tributary of the Yellow River; Luoyang was named after this river
analysis.riverDaduheDesc: 1,062 km long, the largest tributary of the Min River, flowing through western Sichuan — the Luding Bridge spans this river

dock.jumpYearTitle: Click to move the timeline to when this bridge appears
dock.edgeAfterDetail: No more bridges after the current one in the chronological list (year ≤ 1920).
dock.edgeBeforeDetail: No more bridges before the current one in the full chronological list.

load.done: Loaded {count} bridges (rows without coordinates or parsable year were skipped).

warn.noPlayableBridges: [Bridge] No bridges available for playback (need year ≤ 1920 and coordinates within China bounds). Playback stopped — check data or scrub the timeline manually.
